symptoms.symptom_name: Ischaemic rest pain — worse on elevation/supine, relieved by dependency

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Dependency-relieved rest pain is the defining symptom of critical arterial insufficiency.
Venous ulcer pain typically eases with elevation — the opposite pattern.

symptoms.symptom_name: Non-healing punched-out ulcer over the lateral malleolus, enlarging over 6 weeks

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Sharply demarcated 'punched-out' ulcers over bony/pressure points are characteristically arterial.

symptoms.symptom_name: Cold, pale, shiny, hairless lower limb

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Trophic skin changes and a cold foot reflect chronic severe arterial hypoperfusion; patient can report the coldness, the trophic changes are partly video-observable.
Neuropathic feet are typically warm and well-perfused.

symptoms.symptom_name: Dry black eschar (dry gangrene) in the ulcer base

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Necrotic tissue loss defines the limb-threatening category and mandates urgent vascular assessment.

symptoms.symptom_name: Absent dorsalis pedis and posterior tibial pulses; capillary refill > 5 seconds

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absent distal pulses with grossly prolonged capillary refill confirm severe hypoperfusion — a sealed clinician finding; the AI infers ischaemia from the reported cold foot and pain pattern, not a measured pulse.

symptoms.symptom_name: No fever, no spreading erythema, no warmth, no purulence

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of infective features makes superimposed infection unlikely as the current driver, though it remains a safety-net trigger.
Pain-related sympathetic response; non-specific and measured in person, so not available to the AI Doctor over chat.

symptoms.symptom_name: Ankle-brachial pressure index / handheld arterial Doppler

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: ABI and arterial imaging objectively confirm and grade the ischaemia — an in-person/hospital investigation, never available in real time over telehealth.

symptom_clusters.cluster_name: Arterial ulcer / critical limb ischaemia triad
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Dependency-relieved rest pain + cold pale foot + necrotic non-healing ulcer together define limb-threatening ischaemia and mandate urgent vascular assessment.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Ankle-brachial pressure index (ABI) with handheld Doppler
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: First-line objective confirmation of arterial insufficiency — done in person, not achievable over chat.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Urgent arterial duplex ultrasound / CT or MR angiography
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Maps the level and severity of arterial occlusion to plan revascularisation.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Empirical wound swab and antibiotics without infective features
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: No fever, spreading erythema, warmth or purulence — routine swabbing/antibiotics are not indicated and must not delay vascular assessment.
